title: delphinium parryi subsp. maritimum
intro: Delphinium parryi subsp. maritimum, also known as Parry's larkspur, is a rare and unique subspecies of the delphinium plant that grows in coastal areas. This plant contains delphinine, a toxic alkaloid with medicinal properties that can be used to treat various health issues, including pain, inflammation, and fever. The health benefits of Delphinium parryi subsp. maritimum include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful for wound healing and reducing swelling. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and flowers are typically dried and used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, although caution is advised due to its toxic properties.
